questions.text: What is the opening declaration of Surah Al-Mu'minun?
questions.answer: Certainly, the believers have succeeded (Qad aflahal mu'minun).

questions.text: What is the first characteristic of successful believers mentioned in the opening?
questions.answer: They are humble and submissive in their prayers (Khashi'un).

questions.text: How do the successful believers react to 'Laghw' (vain or ill speech)?
questions.answer: They turn away from it and do not engage in it.

questions.text: What is the third quality of successful believers regarding their wealth?
questions.answer: They are observant of the Zakat (obligatory charity).

questions.text: What is the fourth quality of successful believers regarding their chastity?
questions.answer: They guard their private parts, except with their spouses or those their right hands possess.

questions.text: What is the fifth quality of successful believers regarding social responsibility?
questions.answer: They are true to their trusts (Amanat) and their covenants.

questions.text: What is the final quality mentioned in the opening list of successful believers?
questions.answer: They strictly guard their five daily prayers.

questions.text: What is the reward for the believers who possess these seven qualities?
questions.answer: They will inherit Al-Firdaws (the highest Paradise), to dwell therein forever.

questions.text: Describe the creation of man from the earth as mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: Man was created from an extract of clay (Sulalatin min Tin).

questions.text: List the biological stages of human development mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: Nutfah (sperm-drop), then Alaqah (clot), then Mudghah (lump of flesh), then bones, then clothing the bones with flesh, then a new creation.

questions.text: What did Allah create above mankind as 'paths'?
questions.answer: Seven paths (heavens), and He is never unaware of His creation.

questions.text: What are the benefits of the rain sent down by Allah in a measured amount?
questions.answer: It provides water for drinking and for the growth of gardens of date-palms and grapevines with abundant fruit.

questions.text: Which specific tree is mentioned that grows from Mount Sinai and produces oil?
questions.answer: The Olive tree, which provides oil and a relish for those who eat.

questions.text: What lesson is mentioned regarding cattle in this Surah?
questions.answer: In them is a lesson: Allah gives people to drink of what is in their bellies, and they have in them many benefits and food.

questions.text: What is the 'sign' regarding ships and cattle mentioned together?
questions.answer: That you are carried upon ships in the sea and upon cattle on land.

questions.text: Briefly describe the story of Nuh (AS) in this Surah.
questions.answer: He called his people to worship Allah alone, but they accused him of being a mere man seeking superiority and called him mad.

questions.text: What was the command to Nuh (AS) regarding the construction of the Ark?
questions.answer: To construct it 'under Our eyes and Our inspiration' and to take two of every kind along with his family.

questions.text: What prayer was Nuh (AS) told to say once he was settled on the Ark?
questions.answer: 'Praise to Allah who has saved us from the wrongdoing people' and 'My Lord, let me land at a blessed landing place, for You are the best to accommodate [us].'

questions.text: What happened to the 'generations after Nuh' who were given wealth and ease but still rejected their Messengers?
questions.answer: They were seized by the 'Saihah' (blast/shout) in justice and were made into rubbish/scum.

questions.text: Did the various Messengers bring different religions according to this Surah?
questions.answer: No, 'And indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so fear Me.'

questions.text: What is the state of those who give what they give (charity/deeds) while their hearts are 'fearful'?
questions.answer: They are fearful because they know they will return to their Lord, so they hasten to do good deeds.

questions.text: Does Allah charge any soul beyond its capacity?
questions.answer: No, and with Him is a Record which speaks the truth, and they will not be wronged.

questions.text: How did the disbelievers react when they were seized with punishment in this life?
questions.answer: They would cry out for help, but they were told: 'Do not cry out today. Indeed, you will not be helped by Us.'

questions.text: What is the reason the disbelievers rejected the message of the Quran according to the Surah?
questions.answer: They did not ponder the Word, or they found it strange, or they accused the Prophet of being mad, when in fact he brought them the truth they disliked.

questions.text: What would happen if the Truth had followed the desires of the people?
questions.answer: The heavens, the earth, and all who are in them would have been ruined.

questions.text: Did the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) ask for any payment for his message?
questions.answer: No, his Lord's recompense is better, and He is the best of providers.

questions.text: Does suffering always lead the disbelievers to humility?
questions.answer: No, even when Allah seized them with punishment, they did not yield to their Lord, nor did they humble themselves.

questions.text: To whom belong the earth and whoever is in it according to the questioning of the disbelievers?
questions.answer: To Allah, if they only knew.

questions.text: Who is the Lord of the seven heavens and the Lord of the Great Throne?
questions.answer: Allah.

questions.text: In whose hand is the 'Malakut' (sovereignty) of all things, and who protects while none can protect against Him?
questions.answer: Allah.

questions.text: What is the response to those who claim Allah has taken a son or that there are other gods with Him?
questions.answer: Allah has not taken a son, nor is there any god with Him; otherwise, every god would have taken what it created and some would have sought to overcome others.

questions.text: What prayer is the Prophet (SAW) told to make regarding the punishment of the wrongdoers?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, if You should show me that which they are promised, then, my Lord, do not place me among the wrongdoing people.'

questions.text: What is the best way to repel evil according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Repel evil with that which is better.

questions.text: What prayer is given for seeking refuge from the whispers of the devils?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the incitements of the devils, and I seek refuge in You, my Lord, lest they be present with me.'

questions.text: What will the disbeliever say when death comes to him?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, send me back that I might do righteousness in that which I left behind.'

questions.text: What is the 'Barzakh' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: A barrier behind the dead until the Day they are resurrected, preventing their return to the world.

questions.text: Will family ties or kinship matter when the Trumpet is blown?
questions.answer: No, there will be no relationship between them that Day, nor will they ask about each other.

questions.text: What determines success on the Scale (Mizan)?
questions.answer: Those whose scales are heavy with good deeds are the successful; those whose scales are light have lost their souls.

questions.text: What will be the state of the faces of those in Hell?
questions.answer: The Fire will burn their faces, and they will grin therein with their lips displaced.

questions.text: What will be the response to those in Hell when they are asked if the verses were recited to them?
questions.answer: They will admit that they were recited but they denied them, and that their wretchedness overcame them.

questions.text: What will Allah say to the disbelievers when they ask to be taken out of Hell one last time?
questions.answer: 'Remain despised therein and do not speak to Me.'

questions.text: What did the disbelievers use to do to the group of servants who prayed for mercy?
questions.answer: They used to take them in mockery and laugh at them.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who were mocked but remained patient?
questions.answer: Allah has rewarded them this Day for their patience—that they are the attainers [of success].

questions.text: How long will the inhabitants of Hell think they stayed on earth when asked?
questions.answer: They will say, 'We remained a day or part of a day; so ask those who enumerate.'

questions.text: How does Allah respond to the idea that He created mankind without a purpose?
questions.answer: 'Then did you think that We created you in play (uselessly) and that you would not be returned to Us?'

questions.text: What is the final summary of Allah's status at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: He is the True Sovereign (Al-Malik al-Haqq); there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Noble Throne.

questions.text: What happens to someone who invokes another deity along with Allah without proof?
questions.answer: His account is only with his Lord; indeed, the disbelievers will not succeed.

questions.text: What is the final prayer/Ayah of the Surah?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of those who show mercy.'

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Al-Mu'minun'?
questions.answer: The Believers.

questions.text: What is 'Al-Firdaws'?
questions.answer: The highest and best part of Paradise promised to the successful believers.

questions.text: What is the 'extract of clay' symbolic of?
questions.answer: The physical components of the earth from which the human body is formed.

questions.text: What is the 'new creation' (Khalqan akhar) mentioned in the development of the embryo?
questions.answer: It refers to the breathing of the soul into the body and its development into a distinct human being.

questions.text: What is the 'blessed' attribute of Allah mentioned after the stages of creation?
questions.answer: Blessed is Allah, the best of creators.

questions.text: What is the 'measured' rain?
questions.answer: Precipitation that is sent down in the exact amount needed for life without causing total destruction.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention the 'gardens' and 'oil' after the rain?
questions.answer: To show the direct benefits and provision He provides for human survival and enjoyment.

questions.text: What was the 'clear sign' in the cattle?
questions.answer: The production of milk and the various uses they provide for mankind.

questions.text: Why did the chiefs of Nuh's people reject him?
questions.answer: Because he was 'just a human like them' and they thought he wanted to become their leader.

questions.text: What is the 'Shaykhah' (blast)?
questions.answer: A divine sound or vibration used to destroy disobedient nations.

questions.text: What is the 'one religion' (Ummatan Wahidah)?
questions.answer: The core message of Islam (submission to Allah) preached by all prophets.

questions.text: Why do people divide into sects?
questions.answer: Due to pride and following their own interpretations over the clear truth.

questions.text: What is the 'confusion' (Ghamrah) of the disbelievers?
questions.answer: A state of spiritual ignorance and being lost in worldly pursuits.

questions.text: Does wealth and children mean Allah is pleased with someone?
questions.answer: No, they are often a test or a means of gradual leading toward their own end (Istidraj).

questions.text: What is the 'fear' of the righteous?
questions.answer: A deep respect and awe of Allah that makes them cautious about their deeds.

questions.text: What is the 'Record' (Kitab) that speaks the truth?
questions.answer: The comprehensive book of deeds that records everything accurately.

questions.text: What is the 'shouting' (Ja'ar) of the disbelievers when punished?
questions.answer: Their desperate cries for help when it is too late.

questions.text: Why is the Prophet called a 'Warner'?
questions.answer: To alert people to the consequences of their actions and the reality of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'incitement' (Hamazat) of devils?
questions.answer: Their whispers and attempts to stir up evil thoughts and actions in people.

questions.text: What is the 'word' (Kalimah) the disbeliever says at death?
questions.answer: The plea to be sent back, which Allah describes as just a word he says—it has no reality or chance of being granted.

questions.text: What is the 'despised' state in Hell?
questions.answer: A condition of extreme humiliation and distance from Allah's mercy.

questions.text: What is the 'Noble Throne' (Al-Arsh al-Karim)?
questions.answer: The majestic throne of Allah, signifying His honor and power.

questions.text: What is the 'True Promise' mentioned earlier in the Surah?
questions.answer: The inevitability of the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'relish' (Sibgh) of olive oil?
questions.answer: A flavoring and nutritional supplement for food.

questions.text: What is the 'blessed landing place' (Munzalan Mubarakan)?
questions.answer: A place of safety, peace, and abundance after a trial.

questions.text: What is the 'rubbish' (Ghuthah) nations become?
questions.answer: Useless and insignificant remains after their destruction.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention 'hearing, sight, and hearts' together?
questions.answer: As the primary tools for human perception, understanding, and gratitude.

questions.text: What is the 'shadow' of the mountains?
questions.answer: A sign of Allah's control over physical laws.

questions.text: What is the 'straight path' the Prophet calls people to?
questions.answer: The way of life consistent with the truth of Tawhid.

questions.text: What is the 'mercy' requested in the the closing ayah?
questions.answer: Allah's forgiveness and His encompassing kindness.

questions.text: What is the 'success' (Falah) at the end?
questions.answer: Being among those who are saved from the Fire and admitted to Al-Firdaws.
